example_cases.description: detectConflicts returns empty when no conflicts exist
example_cases.input: featureRequirements compatible with profile (same tech stack, no philosophy tension)
example_cases.setup: Create aligned profile and feature requirements

example_cases.input: profile L2 philosophy: 'humans decide, machines execute', feature wants fully automated decision pipeline
example_cases.setup: Create profile with philosophy, feature requirements contradicting it

example_cases.description: detectConflicts includes specific profile and feature entries in report
example_cases.input: Profile constraint: 'REST only', feature requires WebSocket

example_cases.description: detectConflicts finds domain model entity name collision
example_cases.input: profile domain_model entity 'User', feature adds entity also named 'User' with different description
example_cases.setup: Profile L3 with User entity, feature discover with conflicting User entity

example_cases.description: resolveConflict with ADAPT marks conflict resolved without profile update flag
example_cases.expected_output: { resolved: true, action: 'requirement modified to fit', profileUpdateFlag: false }
{ resolved: true, action: 'architecture evolution flagged', profileUpdateFlag: true }

example_cases.description: detectConflicts works with L0/L1 only when L2 disabled
example_cases.input: profile with L0/L1 only (no L2), feature requiring new runtime not in L0
example_cases.expected_output: Detects structural conflict from L0, no philosophy conflict (L2 absent)
example_cases.setup: Profile with L0 only, no L2

property_cases.property: detectConflicts returns a valid ConflictReport regardless of whether L2 exists, is enabled, or is disabled — never throws due to L2 state
detectConflicts reads profile data through MOD-001 API without copying profile content into the conflict report beyond specific quoted entries
